Insight: Effective irradiance (GHI × cloud factor) is the dominant driver of solar generation. Cell temperature derating (via pvlib Faiman model) explains residual forecast variance.
Wind speed is responsible for the majority of forecast variation. Cloud factor indirectly signals atmospheric instability correlated with wind generation.
Combined renewable generation is primarily influenced by effective irradiance and temperature factor. Performance ratio captures systemic losses (inverter, dust, mismatch).
